HFE - inverse marker, presence signals European ancestry.

SLC16A11 - high frequency in Native American populations, associated with diabetes risk.

EUR/MENA Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to Northern and Central Europe, rare across the Mediterranean.
MENA/EUR Tie-Breaker - Frequent in the Levant and Anatolia, drops off sharply in Southern Europe.
NAFR/EUR Tie-Breaker - Distinctive Maghreb marker, filters out false Iberian/Southern European overlap.
AFR/MENA Tie-Breaker - Fixed in Sub-Saharan populations, prevents Middle Eastern noise from registering as African.
MENA/AFR Tie-Breaker - Distinctive to the Arabian Peninsula, filters out false Horn of Africa overlap.
NAFR/AFR Tie-Breaker - Prevents Sahel/Saharan border populations from misclassifying as strictly Sub-Saharan.
EAS/AMR Tie-Breaker - Highly prevalent in Siberia and East Asia, sharply reduced in the Americas.
AMR/EAS Tie-Breaker - Post-migration mutation unique to the Americas, prevents false East Asian trace results.
EAS/AMR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for East Asian coastal populations, effectively absent in Native Americans.
SAS/MENA Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to the Indian subcontinent, prevents misclassification as Iranian/Middle Eastern.
MENA/SAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors populations to the Iranian plateau and West Asia, filtering out South Asian noise.
SAS/EAS Tie-Breaker - Differentiates populations south of the Himalayas from East/Southeast Asian groups.
OCE/EAS Tie-Breaker - Deep Sahul lineage marker, strongly filters out Austronesian/East Asian overlap.
EAS/OCE Tie-Breaker - Common across Southeast Asia, drops sharply in unadmixed Melanesian/Papuan groups.
EUR/SAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Western hunter-gatherer ancestry, drops sharply in South Asian populations.
SAS/EUR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for Ancestral South Indian (ASI) components, effectively absent in Europe.
EAS/SAS Tie-Breaker - Common in Tibeto-Burman populations, filters out false East Asian noise in South Asia.
SAS/EAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Indo-Aryan genetics, strongly separating the subcontinent from East Asia.
NAFR/MENA Tie-Breaker - Deep autochthonous Maghrebi marker, sharply reduces false Middle Eastern overlap.
MENA/NAFR Tie-Breaker - Natufian-derived Levantine marker, preventing Egyptian/North African misclassification.
AFR/OCE Tie-Breaker - Strongly anchors African diversity, completely filtering out Australasian ancient overlap.
OCE/AFR Tie-Breaker - Denisovan-introgressed lineage marker, entirely absent in Sub-Saharan Africa.
AMR/OCE Tie-Breaker - Ancient Beringian mutation, prevents false Papuan/Melanesian signals in Amazonian groups.
OCE/AMR Tie-Breaker - Deep Sahul lineage, effectively absent in indigenous populations of the Americas.
AFR/EAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors deep Sub-Saharan lineages against Southeast Asian introgression.
EAS/AFR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for Austronesian expansion markers, filtering out African overlap.
EUR/EAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Western Eurasian genetics against Siberian/Turkic gene flow.
EAS/EUR Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to Eastern Eurasian Steppe populations.
MENA/SAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Iranian Plateau genetics against Indo-Aryan overlap.
SAS/MENA Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for deeper South Asian (AASI) lineages.
AMR/EUR Tie-Breaker - Strictly isolates Indigenous American DNA from Iberian/European introgression.
EUR/AMR Tie-Breaker - European-specific marker that frequently appears in admixed Latino populations.
NAFR/AFR Tie-Breaker - Anchors indigenous Berber/Maghrebi components against Sub-Saharan gene flow.
AFR/NAFR Tie-Breaker - Sub-Saharan specific marker to filter out noise from the Sahel corridor.
